scores.materiality_reasoning: Property income is a tangible financial asset improving material development.
scores.generality_reasoning: Broad protection of wealth/estate across the duration of exile.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Holds for the ten-year duration of the ostracism period.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The wealthy are a wealth-bracket group, changeable through financial loss.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Plutarch is writing centuries after the Classical Athenian practice of ostracism.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports a specific legal clause of the ostracism statute as a fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Exile usually involves confiscation; income retention is a specific legal addition.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Ritual participation is primarily symbolic/ceremonial with weak material payoff.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a specific recurring civic ritual in Plataea.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Resource attached to a recurring annual or periodic festival.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slave status is a structural birth-status requiring legal manumission to change.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to a ritual established after the Battle of Plataea (479 BCE).
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes the ritual prohibition as an observed, factual protocol.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Lack of ritual access is not part of the definition of slavery.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The archonship carries direct legal and administrative power over the state.
scores.generality_reasoning: Access to high public office is a broad political resource.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Eligibility is a permanent-till-revoked status for the citizen body.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a structural legal status determined by birth.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes a 5th-century BCE legislative change in Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Names a specific historical decree (Aristides' decree) as a fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Eligibility for high office is not inherent in the definition of a citizen.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Financial dowry and public sustenance are tangible material wealth.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specifically tied to the marriage procedure and provision for children.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Dowries provide lifelong material security for the women involved.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Being the daughter of a specific individual is biologically fixed.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Concerns the descendants of Aristides in the 5th century BCE.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported as a customary action of the city, citing an established story.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Public provision of dowries is an external state right, not a definition.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Military education leads to professional roles with tangible outcomes.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational societal axis for Roman youth development.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Resource is bounded to the life stage of youth/young adulthood.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Age (minors) is biologically fixed at a given moment.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the Roman Republic (2nd Punic War) era.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes a general societal practice as factual historical context.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Military training is a societal right, not part of the definition of 'minor'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Eligibility for consulship allows holding the highest material power in Rome.
scores.generality_reasoning: Major recognizable right to high public office.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Eligibility is recurring per cycle but depends on checking age each time.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Age is a biologically fixed trait at any specific time.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes legislative opposition and legal norms of the 2nd century BCE.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites the specific laws of age and the cursus honorum as facts.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Age requirements are external legal constraints, not definitions of youth.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Exemption from tax and associated imprisonment is a direct material effect.
scores.generality_reasoning: Right to avoid a specific recurring tax (the metoikion).
scores.persistence_reasoning: The tax is a continuous long-term fiscal obligation for residents.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Metic status is a durable identity based on residency and foreign birth.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes a historical anecdote involving 4th-century BCE figures.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Refers to the 'alien tax' as an established legal fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Paying a tax is a legal duty, not the definition of a foreign resident.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to wear a specific cap is a symbolic marker of status.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a specific ritual (triumph) and a particular item of clothing.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Associated with the single episode of the triumphal procession.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Refers to slaves in the process of becoming freedmen, a legal status change.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes a specific 2nd-century BCE Roman triumph.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes the custom as a factual part of the manumission procedure.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Wearing a felt cap is a symbolic right, not the definition of a slave.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Senatorial rank is a concrete legal right with massive material influence.
scores.generality_reasoning: A broad category of power and institutional membership.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Rank is permanent-till-revoked by a censor.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Nobility/consular dignity is a structural birth and career status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the historical censorship of Cato the Elder.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes the censor's power to purge the senate as a legal fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Retention of rank is not part of the definition of having reached that rank.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Priority seating is a ceremonial honor with minor material benefit.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a particular institution (the theater) and setting.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Resource attached to a recurring civic event (theater spectacles).
scores.immutability_reasoning: The Senate is a stable structural body involving legal/birth status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes a specific historical incident in a Roman theater.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites the seating priority as 'according to custom', a factual norm.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Theater seating is a status marker, not part of the definition of 'senator'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Citizenship provides direct material and legal rights (tax/voting).
scores.generality_reasoning: Citizenship is a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Once granted, citizenship is generally a lifelong structural status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The group is defined by parentage (born of free parents), which is immutable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the censorship of Flamininus and Marcellus.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Refers to the enrollment criteria as a factual policy.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizenship is the reward; being 'born of free parents' is the condition.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Kingship involves supreme control over national resources and military.
scores.generality_reasoning: Kingship is the highest foundational axis of political power in Sparta.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The office of King was a lifelong structural position.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Descent from an 'ancient family' is biologically/lineage fixed.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discusses the historical Spartan constitution and the rule of Lysander.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The author argues a point but acknowledges the factual norm of descent.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being of a specific lineage and being the King are distinct concepts.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Marriage and sumptuary laws have direct material effects on spending/wealth.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers a broad class of life outcomes (expenditure and family law).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Legislation imposes long-term constraints while the laws remain in force.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a hard-to-change legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the dictatorship and legislation of Sulla.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports Sulla's enactment of laws as a historical fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a citizen does not inherently define one's marriage laws.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Right to property/estates is a tangible material wealth resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Wealth/property is a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Loss of an estate through confiscation is a permanent/structural loss.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The wealthy are defined by a wealth bracket, which can change.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the proscriptions/confiscations during Sulla's rule.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes the public auction and confiscation of estates as factual events.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Retaining property is not part of the definition of being wealthy.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Protecting life (freedom from execution) is the most material resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Bodily integrity/right to life is a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The loss of life is a permanent/lifelong structural outcome.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a legal status; 'sons of consuls' is lineage-based (immutable).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the period of civil strife under Sulla.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported as historical atrocities that occurred in defiance of laws.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Right to life is not part of the definition of citizenship.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Daily sustenance (meals) provided material means of subsistence.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specifically tied to the institution of the public/private food distribution.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Daily sustenance is a recurring resource while the patron remains active.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Nobles (aristocratic natures) were structural birth-statuses.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes 5th-century BCE practices in Cimonian Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the 'table of Cimon' as a well-known historical charitable practice.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Redistributing wealth is not part of the definition of nobility.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Ostracism has a direct material effect through removal of political rights.
scores.generality_reasoning: Affects broad political personhood and freedom of residency.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Exile lasts for a specified long-term period (ten years).
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a structural legal status determined by birth.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the Classical Athenian ostracism mechanism.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites the mechanism and duration (ten years) as a factual law.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The power to exile is not part of the definition of 'citizen'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Supreme military command involves control over vast armies and territory.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational societal axis of political/military power.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Command is long-term, covering entire campaigns or seasons of office.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The group comprises the ruling elite/aristocracy (nobles).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to historical commands of Cimon and Lucullus.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Refers to the country 'conferring imperial power' as a factual procedure.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a noble does not automatically define one as an imperial commander.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Kingship provides total control over a state's resources and military.
scores.generality_reasoning: Kingship is the highest foundational axis of political power in Sparta.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Spartan kingship was a lifelong, hereditary position.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Belonging to the Eurypontidae family is biologically/lineage fixed.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the historical Spartan royal succession.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites the lineage requirement for kingship as a factual structural rule.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The resource (kingship) is distinct from the group definition (lineage members).

scores.materiality_reasoning: Kingship provides supreme political and material power in the state.
scores.generality_reasoning: Kingship is the foundational axis of Spartan political structure.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Kingship is a lifelong structural office.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Refers to the Eurypontidae (lineage), a biologically fixed group.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to historical Spartan requirements and oracular laws.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Names 'sound of limb' as a factual legal/religious requirement.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Physical soundness is not part of the definition of the Eurypontidae family.
